3c:["$","p",null,{"className":"x:not-first:mt-[1.25em] x:leading-7","children":["A few details of the class-type form follow the ",["$","a",null,{"href":"https://github.com/samchon/typia/blob/master/packages/interface/src/typings/Classifiable.ts","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"x:focus-visible:nextra-focus x:text-primary-600 x:underline x:hover:no-underline x:decoration-from-font x:[text-underline-position:from-font]","children":[["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"Classifiable<T>"}],false]}]," contract. A constructor or factory counts only when it takes a single meaningful argument: a first parameter that may be optional, with every later parameter optional or rest. A rest-only parameter ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"(...xs: S[])"}]," takes the element ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"S"}]," as its seed, not the array, and a rest ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"tuple"}]," ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"(...xs: [P, Q?])"}]," takes its first element ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"P"}],". A ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"from"}]," is a factory only when it returns the instance, so a divergent factory like ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"Temporal.PlainDate.from(isoString)"}]," is honored while a helper ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"from(text): number"}]," is not. A ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"from(json: any)"}]," collapses to field copy — the seed shape can’t be ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"any"}],". An ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"abstract"}]," class exposes no runnable constructor, so it field-copies rather than ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"new"}],"-ing. A class nested inside a seed is itself classified (method-stripped, then field-copied), so the whole graph comes back as instances. For a class-type target the validated variants check the ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"seed"}],", not the class’s static members."]}]
3d:["$","p",null,{"className":"x:not-first:mt-[1.25em] x:leading-7","children":["One constraint is enforced at transform time: a class with ES ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"#private"}]," fields cannot be field-copied, because ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"Object.create"}]," + assign never installs the private slots and the prototype’s methods would then throw ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"Cannot read private member"}],". classify therefore rejects such a class at any field-copied position — a nested class, or a root with no construction strategy — with a compile-time error; give it a single-argument constructor or a static ",["$","code",null,{"className":"nextra-code","dir":"ltr","children":"from"}]," so it is ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"constructed"}]," instead."]}]
